channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: War Risk for Businesses Will Mean Higher Prices No Matter What Happens
item.description: Doing business in a more dangerous and unpredictable world will cost more, pushing up the price of everything from food to electronics.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supply chain disruptions have raised costs and disrupted manufacturing across Southeast Asia.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Hit Greater Tunb Island in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military said on Wednesday it had attacked Iranian cruise missile storage and launch sites on Greater Tunb, one of several small islands in the Strait of Hormuz subject to a decades-old territorial dispute.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Vessels at anchor in May off the coast of Oman, about 60 miles from Greater Tunb island in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: For Israel, the U.S.-Iran Hostilities Have Created an Uneasy Limbo
item.description: Officials see a return to full-blown war as preferable to an agreement that fails to curb the threats Iran poses to Israel. Meanwhile, they wait.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Israelis gathering near a public shelter in Tel Aviv after a warning of incoming missiles from Iran in March.

item.title: Trump’s Latest Nominee for C.D.C. Director Will Face Senate Panel
item.description: Dr. Erica Schwartz supports vaccines and is seen as a mainstream choice to lead an agency that has been a target of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s efforts to change federal immunization policies.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
item.media:description: The Senate health committee is expected to approve Dr. Erica Schwartz’s candidacy and send it to the full chamber for confirmation.

item.title: Trump Pressures ICE to Resume Traffic Stops After They Were Halted Over Fatal Shootings
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement ordered its officers on Tuesday to halt most vehicle stops across the country after they shot two people over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: In a post on social media on Wednesday, President Trump defended the traffic stops and urged ICE to “go back and do your very important job.”

item.title: Colombian Immigrant Killed by ICE in Maine Had Legal Status, Father Says
item.description: The father of Joan Sebastian Guerrero, the man fatally shot by a federal immigration agent on Monday, said his son had been working two jobs to support his wife and daughter.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales, Jenna Russell and Jacey Fortin
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial for Joan Sebastian Guerrero at the intersection where he was shot by ICE in Biddeford, Maine.

item.title: Videos Detail Moments Surrounding Fatal ICE Shooting in Biddeford, Maine
item.description: Footage obtained by The New York Times shows ICE agents in the moments before and after the killing of Joan Sebastian Guerrero. The circumstances of the shooting remain unclear.
item.dc:creator: Allison McCann, Devon Lum, Tim Ouillette, Aric Toler and Leanne Abraham
item.media:credit: Cory Poulin and Sadie Dilboy, via Reuters

item.title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
item.description: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva, Marc Santora and Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:credit: Commander of Unmanned Aerial Systems Force, via Reuters
item.media:description: A screenshot taken from footage provided by Ukraine’s Unmanned Systems Forces showed a drone flying toward a ship at port in the city of Kerch in Crimea in early July.

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Passion in Every Frame
item.description: Few directors close the divide between the art film and the blockbuster like Christopher Nolan.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Archaeologists Unearth a 2,400-Year-Old Sanctuary for the Worship of Odysseus
item.description: The ruins of a 2,400-year-old complex suggest that, to the Greeks of antiquity, Homer’s protagonist was not just a character in a poem but a figure of genuine devotion.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz
item.media:credit: Fine Art Images/Heritage Images, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A 19th-century painting by the Swiss painter Arnold Böcklin depicting Odysseus fleeing the Cyclops Polyphemus.

item.title: House G.O.P. Releases Budget to Unlock $95 Billion for Iran War and SAVE Act
item.description: The plan faces a rocky path amid Republican divisions and would pave the way for votes months before the midterm elections to fund a war that polls show is deeply unpopular.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Jodey Arrington, Republican of Texas and the chairman of the House Budget Committee. His panel was set to vote on the plan on Thursday.

item.title: A Year After DOGE Cuts, Social Security Is Trying to Stabilize
item.description: The agency has been quietly hiring workers again, and its leadership is focused on improving technology to ease workloads left by the departure of about 7,800 staff members.
item.dc:creator: Tara Siegel Bernard
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Social Security Administration has reassigned many of its workers to staff its national 800 number, an effort to shorten wait times.

item.title: Cyclospora Was Already Hard to Investigate. Funding Cuts Make It Harder.
item.description: The parasite, which has sickened thousands across the U.S., is notoriously difficult to track. Funding cuts haven’t made the job easier.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Hopkins Legaspi
item.media:credit: Jae Young Ju/Getty Images

item.title: How to Stay Safe From Cyclospora, a Parasite Causing Severe Intestinal Illness
item.description: Cyclospora, a parasite that causes intestinal illness, is sickening many people across the United States. Health experts have tips for avoiding infection.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Yana Iskayeva/Getty Images

item.title: He Thought He Had to Break Out of Brooklyn. Instead, He Celebrates It.
item.description: As a child, Michael McLeer wanted to be like John Travolta in “Saturday Night Fever” and leave the neighborhood. A tragedy changed his mind.
item.dc:creator: Abby Ellin and Lanna Apisukh

item.title: Should Epstein’s Friends Be Punished? An Elite Harvard Club Is Torn.
item.description: At Harvard, students, alumni and professors debate whether a storied campus group should cut ties with one of Jeffrey Epstein’s closest associates, and whether Harvard itself should do the same.
item.dc:creator: Debra Kamin
item.media:credit: Jemal Countess/Getty Images for The Hasty Pudding Institute of 1770
item.media:description: Andrew Farkas spoke in 2014 at an event for the Hasty Pudding Institute, a Harvard group. Mr. Farkas has come under criticism for soliciting funds for the group from Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Goldman’s Former Top Lawyer Calls Epstein a ‘Masterful Liar’
item.description: Kathryn Ruemmler, the former general counsel of Goldman Sachs, testified on Wednesday in a closed-door congressional hearing investigating Jeffery Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: William B. Plowman/NBCUniversal, via Getty Images

item.title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
item.description: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Senator Mark Kelly Amasses Nearly $25 Million Campaign War Chest
item.description: The Arizona Democrat said he had raised and given away $10 million to fellow members of his party and committees as he mulls a presidential run in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Mark Kelly’s fund-raising jumped last year after President Trump accused him and five other Democrats of “sedition” over a video in which they told service members they could ignore illegal orders.

item.title: Elon Musk Funds Vivek Ramaswamy’s Bid for Ohio Governor Despite Their DOGE Clashes
item.description: Elon Musk gave $5 million to back Vivek Ramaswamy’s campaign for Ohio governor, seemingly moving past tensions around the effort to downsize the federal work force.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times; Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy developed a frosty relationship after President Trump named them as the leaders of the so-called Department of Government Efficiency.

item.title: Biden Will Release Memoir Just After the November Midterms
item.description: The former president announced that the book, “Promise Me, America,” will come out on Nov. 17, and will cover the tumultuous events of his presidency as well as his re-election bid.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Little, Brown and Co., via Associated Press
item.media:description: Former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. said his book would cover topics including how he handled the coronavirus pandemic, the economy, conflicts in Afghanistan and Ukraine, and “restoring our democracy.”

item.title: The American E.V. Has Been Crushed. Will It Take the U.S. Auto Industry With It?
item.description: The largest U.S. automakers have backed away from electric vehicles, even as global sales are booming. The decision may make them obsolete.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Shaer

item.title: We’re About to Find Out Whether Republican Senators Can Still Say No
item.description: Republicans should have the decency, patriotism and self-respect to vote against Mr. Blanche.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board
item.media:credit: Pete Keihart for The New York Times

item.title: How Worried Should You Be About Cyclospora?
item.description: Don’t stop eating fruits and vegetables.
item.dc:creator: Emily Oster
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: Fatal Encounters
item.description: We look at shootings by federal immigration agents.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial for Joan Sebastian Guerrero.

item.title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
item.description: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Laura Proctor/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The CN Tower in Toronto on Wednesday as smoke from wildfires enveloped the city.
